results.title: Data Scientist Intern | Hirevector
highlights: a part of the global industrial organization Marmon Holdings—which is backed by Berkshire Hathaway— you’ll be doing things that matter, leading at every level, and winning a better way. We’re committed to making a positive impact on the world, providing you with diverse learning and working opportunities, and fostering a culture where everyone’s empowered to be their best. A Summer Experience. A Lifetime of Value. We’ll meet you where you are and help you go further . This summer, we’re committed to bringing early-in-career talent together, trust you to own your work and help you level up through professional development, networking, and exposure to real-world projects. We’re doing things that matter . Energizing North America: That’s the impact of your work at Cerrowire. Copper building wire doesn’t just supply electricity. It turns houses into homes. It allows hospitals to deliver the care patients need. It keeps every single industry we rely on up and running. And it all starts with you. As a part of Marmon, your impact also goes way beyond North America. You’re helping keep millions around the world healthy, connected and safe. Join the Biggest Small Business You’ll Ever Find. Headquartered in Hartselle, Alabama, Cerrowire is a copper wire manufacturing company with plants in Alabama, Georgia, Indiana, and Utah. We build, energize, and inspire by supplying building wire and cable throughout North America for commercial, industrial and residential use. We bring power to life. Cerrowire is hiring talented and innovative interns looking to power up their futures and forge a meaningful career with us. Be part of our efforts to help improve the quality of life for millions of people by engineering solutions to many of the world’s greatest needs. You will gain substantial personal learning and career development opportunities through ownership of real-world job assignments, strong coaching and mentorship, and networking opportunities with senior leaders and other interns across our organization. Our interns receive the opportunity to showcase their achievements to our Leaders for the opportunity to grow and make an impact in the current workplace, across a group, and around the world. At Cerrowire, a Marmon company, you get the best of both worlds. The strength and stability that comes with being part of Berkshire Hathaway, plus the autonomy and opportunity that comes with working at one of our 120+ companies. What You’ll Do: The Data Scientist Intern will help develop predictive machine learning models to optimize scheduling for maintenance and shipping, launch a RAG LLM for digitizing SOPs, and support workforce digital literacy training.. They will get on-hand experience collaborating with stakeholders and presenting to executives as well as mentorship from senior leaders. What You’ll Need: Pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree in Computer Science or related field Rising Junior or Senior Preferred experience with Python, Tensorflow, PyTorch, Git, or related tools Excellent written, verbal communication and presentation skills Ability to effectively communicate issues and solutions to all levels of the team Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Compensation: $22.00 - $26.00 per hour, commensurate with relevant experience and educational background Work Hours/Length of Program: The internship will run for 12 weeks from May to August Full Time, targeting 40 hours per week (possibility for more flexible hours and Part Time if necessary) Exact start and end dates are flexible based on school schedules and the needs of the business This is a paid internship Working Conditions and Physical Demands: This position is based in our Corporate Office, and employees are expected to wear business casual dress. The office is connected to a state-of-the-art manufacturing facility and may involve general exposure to a manufacturing environment, including exposure to noise, temperature fluctuations,

results.title: Data Scientist - Specialty Equipment Services | Hirevector
highlights: " expertise to help you succeed. With over 30 specialty and property and casualty operations, there are always opportunities here to learn and grow. At Great American, we value and recognize the benefits derived when people with different backgrounds and experiences work together to achieve business results. Our goal is to create a workplace where all employees feel included, empowered, and enabled to perform at their best. The Specialty Equipment Services division is looking for a Data Scientist to build a modern data intelligence team that improves decisions, strengthens client value, and increases operational efficiency. The data scientist role blends strategy and hands-on delivery: you will partner with leaders to define the right questions, map current data realities, and deliver analytics and AI solutions that help us tell our story and drive results for both our clients and Great American Insurance. Specialty Equipment - Great American Insurance Group Great American’s culture is built on connection, shared learning, and strong relationships. To support this, employees in this role are expected to be on‑site four days a week, with the flexibility to work one day remotely. Core in‑office days are Tuesday–Thursday, with the fourth day determined by business needs. Essential Job Functions & Responsibilities Work closely with leadership team to identify key metrics and insights needed to support strategic goals and business objectives Develop and maintain financial models for large commercial clients and internal underwriting and leadership stakeholders Translate complex account structures, underwriting economics, and performance drivers into clear narratives and visuals; a storyteller mindset Perform gap analysis on current state, develop and recommend roadmap sequence of investments (projects, processes, technology enhancements) to achieve vision Drive innovation by exploring new tools, alternate data sources, modeling techniques to enhance business performance Lead development of an “insight” digital product for clients Guide training to promote data literacy skillsets for divisional employees Works closely with divisional leaders, shared services, and IT partners to ensure solutions are practical and adoptable Job Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field (or equivalent experience) Generally, 3 or more years of experience translating business needs into analytics deliverables, measurables, and compelling narratives Strong analytical and problem-solving skills; ability to structure ambiguous problems and define success metrics Working knowledge of modern data practices (data sourcing, data quality, modeling, and governance concepts) Proficiency with analytical tools and languages appropriate for data science work (e.g., SQL and at least one modeling/scripting language) Ability to communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders; strong presentation and writing skills. May require periodic travel to stakeholder sites or company locations (10% Travel) Preferred Qualifications Experience in insurance, financial services, or risk-oriented businesses Experience building client-facing analytics or ‘insight’ products Familiarity with cloud data platforms and BI tools used in the organization (e.g., Snowflake, Tableau, Power BI) Experience piloting and operationalizing AI use cases in partnership with IT and business teams Business Unit: Specialty Equipment Benefits: We offer competitive benefits packages for full-time and part-time employees*. Full-time employees have access to medical, dental, and vision coverage, wellness plans, parental leave, adoption assistance, and tuition reimbursement. Full-time and eligible part-time employees also enjoy Paid Time Off and paid holidays, a 401(k) plan with company match, an employee stock purchase plan, and commuter benefits. results.title: Agentic AI Data Scientist | Hirevector
highlights: Data Scientist - GD08AE We’re determined to make a difference and are proud to be an insurance company that goes well beyond coverages and policies. Working here means having every opportunity to achieve your goals – and to help others accomplish theirs, too. Join our team as we help shape the future. Step into the future with The Hartford as an Agentic AI Data Scientist, where Generative AI isn't just a buzzword—it's central to our strategy. Join a team that's pioneering AI-driven solutions in underwriting, claims processing, and customer service, transforming how we operate and make decisions. As part of our Global Specialty Data Science & Analytics team, you'll tackle advanced use cases and shape the development of Gen AI capabilities across the organization. Here, your work will have a tangible impact, driving innovation and operational excellence with cutting-edge AI tools. If you're looking for a role where your expertise is not only valued but essential to our success, The Hartford is the place for you. We are looking for a technically proficient GenAI data scientist with deep implementation experience in building and evaluating GenAI systems, and a proven ability to stay at the forefront tools and techniques in the evolving AI landscape. This role can have a Hybrid or Remote work schedule. Candidates who live near one of our locations will have the expectation of working in an office 3 days a week (Tuesday through Thursday). Responsibilities: Support The Hartford’s AI innovation strategy to identify and implement high-end use cases, ensuring alignment with business objectives and emerging industry trends. Support cutting-edge AI vision and roadmap for the enterprise to make The Hartford a leader in using AI for the insurance industry. Partner with stakeholders internal to the Data Science organization, Technology organization and business organization, aligning with business objectives and driving innovation to improve productivity and efficiency of underwriting and sales efforts. Design and build GenAI solutions for tasks such as summarization, question answering, search, data synthesis, etc. Apply AI techniques to low frequency, high severity exposures in Specialty Insurance. Rapidly prototype with cutting-edge tools, such as VertexAI/Google agent development kit, LangChain/LangGraph, RAG frameworks, HuggingFace, OpenAI APIs, etc. Research and recommend noval foundation model options, tools, and libraries to improve solutions in performance and reliability. Develop evaluation pipelines for generative outputs using a mix of automatic metrics, human feedback, and llm-as-a-judge methods. Collaborate with ML Ops to ensure best practice is followed for scalable training and deployment. Qualifications: Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Engineering or a related field. 3+ years of industry experience in machine learning or data science and with 1+ years focused on GenAI. Experience developing and deploying agentic AI workflows and systems. Proven experience in GenAI tools such as Vertex AI/Google agent development kit, LangChain/LangGraph, RAG frameworks, HuggingFace, OpenAI APIs, etc. Solid understanding of prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), agent workflow, and LLM evaluation. Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, etc. Experience with vector databases such as Chroma, Pinecone, etc. and cloud platforms such as GCP, AWS, Azure, etc. Strong analytical, problem solving and debugging skills. Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders across the enterprise. Candidate must be authorized to work in the US without company sponsorship. The company will not support the STEM OPT I-983 Training Plan endorsement for this position. Compensation The listed annualized base pay range is primarily based on analysis of similar positions in the

# Berkshire Hathaway Data Scientist Salary

The average Berkshire Hathaway Data Scientist earns an estimated $138,565 annually, which includes an estimated base salary of $117,228 with a $21,337 bonus. Berkshire Hathaway's Data Scientist compensation is $9,060 more than the US average for a Data Scientist. Data Scientist salaries at Berkshire Hathaway can range from $47,500 - $280,000.

The Engineering Department at Berkshire Hathaway earns $5,467 more on average than the Marketing Department.

As a Lead Generative AI Specialist, you will be responsible for: Designing and developing of generative AI models, including but not limited to LLMs, VLMs, GANs and Multimodal LLMs. Conducting and driving forward innovative research in the field of generative AI, leading the team in exploring new methodologies and technologies. Working closely with cross-functional teams to integrate new generative models into existing AI systems, ensuring seamless integration and optimizing performance. Developing and testing software components according to customer and technical requirements. Preparing comprehensive reports and presentations on the impact of generative AI initiatives on the business. PhD is preferred Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work across different disciplines. Demonstrated skill at function in a team setting Demonstrated awareness of critical thinking and problem solving methods Demonstrated awareness of presentation and influencing skills Ability to analyze and resolve problems through innovation and creative solutions. Work in a way that works for you We recognize that everyone is different and that the way in which people want to work and deliver at their best is different for everyone too. We know we are better when all of our people are developed, engaged and able to bring their whole authentic selves to work. We invest in the health and well-being of our workforce, train and reward talent and develop leaders at all levels to bring out the best in each other. Working for you Our inventions have revolutionized energy for over a century. But to keep going forward tomorrow, we know we have to push the boundaries today. AI Data Scientist | Jobflarely

Jobflarely.← All Jobs

# AI Data Scientist

Posted Jun 9, 2026

Burlington Stores, Inc. is a growing company committed to being an employer of choice. They are seeking a Junior AI Data Scientist to support various business areas by analyzing data and developing AI-driven solutions to enhance retail operations and customer experiences.

### Responsibilities

- Work with stakeholders throughout the organization including Merchandising, Allocations, Marketing, IT and Supply Chain Analytics to assist in developing and implementing AI-powered solutions
- Mine and analyze structured and unstructured data from the data warehouse
- Support prompt engineering and optimization for large language models (LLMs)
- Integrate AI services via APIs and cloud AI platforms
- Keep up to date with latest technology trends
- Work closely with senior data scientists to expand technical and functional knowledge as well as apply best practices
- Coordinate with different functional teams to identify AI automation opportunities
- Develop processes and tools to monitor and analyze model performance and data accuracy
- Translate complex analytical findings into clear business recommendations

Skills

- Bachelor's degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science, or related field
- 0–2 years of experience in AI, data analytics, or related technical role
- Knowledge in Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and AI model evaluation concepts
- Exposure to cloud AI platforms (Azure AI, AWS AI services, or GCP Vertex AI)
- 2+ years Data Science experience with extensive background in building ML models, data mining and statistical analysis
- Strong interpersonal and relationship building skills
- Strong written and verbal communication skills

### Benefits

- Competitive wage and benefit package
- Generous paid time off plan
- Company matched 401(k)
- Associate discount
- Medical, dental and vision coverage
- Life and disability insurance
- Paid holidays
- Training and development opportunities

Company Overview

- Burlington Stores, Inc., headquartered in New Jersey, is a nationally recognized off-price retailer. It was founded in 1972, and is headquartered in Burlington, New Jersey, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is https://www.burlington.com/. Company H1B Sponsorship

- Burlington Stores, Inc. has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 2 in 2026, 4 in 2025, 2 in 2024, 7 in 2023, 7 in 2022, 4 in 2021, 4 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: Berkshire CEO has sobering message for tech stock investors - AOL
highlights:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A, BRK.B) CEO Greg Abel isn’t chasing the AI trade just because everyone else is.
[...]
In his first annual meeting as Berkshire’s top honcho and Warren Buffett’s successor, he said that the company isn’t treating tech spending or tech stocks as a momentum game.
[...]
It has the firepower to make massive tech stock bets and lean aggressively into AI.
[...]
Instead, Abel drew a different line at Berkshire’s 2026 annual meeting on May 2.
[...]
“We're going to be a builder of technology, rather than just a buyer of technology,” he told shareholders.
[...]
“It has to be additive to our businesses. We're not going to have AI just to have AI.”
[...]
Berkshire Hathaway’s new CEO Greg Abel signaled a disciplined approach to technology, prioritizing long-term value over AI-driven spending hypeDaniel Zuchnik/WireImage
[...]
### Takeaways from CEO Greg Abel’s technology message
[...]
Technology should complement, not be a fix-on: Abel noted that tech needs to truly add value to a business, rather than simply serve as a sticker signaling AI use.
[...]
Buyer mindset versus builder mindset: Berkshire is looking to go in-house with AI solutions, such as the predictive maintenance effort at BNSF Railway, rather than leaving innovation to others.
[...]
## Berkshire keeps technology spending disciplined
[...]
Abel essentially drew a line between useful tech tools and expensive hype.
[...]
“The entire Berkshire franchise is touched by it,” he said, noting that tech is actually playing a key role across the company’s businesses.
[...]
For instance, its insurance units study predictive analytics, while BNSF uses data to consistently improve rail operations.
[...]
Nevertheless, Berkshire hasn’t by any means rushed into major AI face-lift projects or related investments just because the market’s been obsessed with the technology.
[...]
We can see that discipline from BNSF and its most recent report.
[...]
, while operating margins improved
[...]
the back of higher volumes
[...]
better productivity.

Cofounder, Aimpoint Digital

2d Edited

As our Anthropic business continues to grow, we’re hiring a Forward Deployed AI Engineer - Claude, in the US. We’re looking for a senior individual contributor who is already living and breathing Claude AI solution engineering. Clients often find us after being pitched the "sexy stuff," with a desire to understand what is "real" and practical in the context of their businesses. This is a hands-on role, with example activities below: - Breaking down value chain activities from first principles, decoupling the workflows, business/technical decisions, handoffs, and operational controls that Claude can augment, automate, or re-engineer. - Rationalizing Claude deployment strategies across an organization to improve operational efficacy, governance, cost control, and data security (experience with Databricks, hyperscalers, or Snowflake is highly desirable). - Designing AND THEN building agentic workflows that integrate with enterprise systems, homegrown applications, tools, and human-in-the-loop operating models. - Establishing evaluation frameworks, guardrails, and reliability patterns so that our solutions can move from prototype to production with measurable results (this is critical as majority of organizations are still struggling with operationalization - you will be putting Claude-engineered solutions into production at Aimpoint). - Translating ambiguous business problems into effectively scoped technical architectures, working solutions, and measurable testing frameworks that validate solution viability and support value-based contracting. If this sounds fun and you have deep hands-on experience with Claude, agentic workflows, evaluation, tool use, RAG, and enterprise AI implementation, we’d love to pitch you on building with us! Apply here: https://lnkd.in/dGun6QGm

Job Title: AI Engineer / Generative AI Engineer 📍 Location: Texas (TX) – Hybrid/Onsite 💼 Employment Type: W2 Only ✅ Independent Visa Holders Only 🧑💻 Experience: 5–6 Years Please Find the Below Requirement If You’re Interested, Please Share Me your Updated Resume To rakeshmanda@adroitinnovative.com Job Description We are seeking a highly skilled AI Engineer with Generative AI expertise to design, develop, and deploy enterprise-grade AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience building LLM-powered applications, RAG pipelines, AI Agents, and production-ready GenAI solutions using modern AI frameworks and cloud technologies. Required Skills 5–6 years of Software Development experience with strong Python programming. 2+ years of hands-on experience in Generative AI and Large Language Models (LLMs). Strong experience with OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, Llama, Hugging Face, or similar foundation models. Experience designing and implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures. Hands-on experience with LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Semantic Kernel, CrewAI, or AutoGen. Experience with Vector Databases such as Pinecone, Weaviate, Chroma, FAISS, or Milvus. Strong understanding of Prompt Engineering, Embeddings, Fine-Tuning, and Model Evaluation. Experience building AI Agents and Agentic AI workflows. Experience with REST APIs, FastAPI, and microservices architecture. Knowledge of AWS, Azure, or GCP cloud platforms. Familiarity with Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, and MLOps practices. Responsibilities Design, develop, and deploy GenAI applications using LLMs and Agentic AI frameworks. Build scalable RAG pipelines leveraging vector databases and enterprise knowledge sources. Develop AI-powered chatbots, copilots, and intelligent assistants. Implement prompt engineering strategies and optimize model performance. Integrate LLM solutions with enterprise applications and APIs. Develop and maintain AI evaluation frameworks for quality, accuracy, latency, and hallucination reduction. Collaborate with business and engineering teams to identify AI use cases and deliver solutions. Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize AI applications in production environments. Follow responsible AI practices, security standards, and governance requirements
